title: BGLS convexity theorem (thm:bgls) is sketched, not proved: compound/exterior hyperbolic construction asserted

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The deepest analytic input of the proof is Theorem `thm:bgls` (Bauschke--Güler--Lewis--Sendov): for a homogeneous polynomial $f$ hyperbolic in direction $e$ with eigenvalues $\lambda_1(z)\ge\dots\ge\lambda_d(z)$, each partial sum $\sigma_k(z)=\sum_{i=1}^k\lambda_i(z)$ is a CONVEX function of $z$.

The attempt's 'proof sketch' reduces this to convexity of the LARGEST eigenvalue (Gårding) of a **compound polynomial** $f^{[k]}$ that is 'built from the action of $f$ on the $k$-th exterior power, whose largest eigenvalue equals $\sigma_k(z)$.' The existence of $f^{[k]}$ and its required properties — homogeneity, hyperbolicity in some direction $e^{[k]}$, and the eigenvalue identity $\lambda_1(f^{[k]})(z)=\sigma_k(z)$ — are precisely the technical heart of BGLS and are NOT established here; they are asserted with a citation.

This is load-bearing: `cor:psd` (PSD-ness of $\sum_i\gamma_iH^{(i)}$, the crux of the contraction Proposition) depends entirely on $\operatorname{Hess}\sigma_k\succeq0$. The document also frames itself as 'self-contained up to Gårding,' which is inaccurate given this gap.

Resolution options: (a) explicitly treat BGLS as a cited theorem with exact statement and reference (then this becomes a framing fix, not a hole); or (b) prove the compound-hyperbolic construction, e.g. via Brändén's stable-polynomial machinery, or via a determinantal/symmetric-matrix representation reducing to Ky Fan's classical convexity of $\sum_{i\le k}\lambda_i$ and a Gårding-cone limiting argument.
